events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a back-graded section of drain piping that fails a puddle test, observed at rough-complete stage. IPC 704 covers drainage slope requirements - drains must slope properly (typically 1/4" per foot for pipes 3" and smaller, 1/8" per foot for 4" and larger). A back-graded section means the pipe slopes the wrong way or is flat/back-graded, causing water to puddle. This is non-compliant.

Decision: fail
Risk: high (standing water in drains, sewage backup risk, health hazard)
Work stage: rough-complete
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: maybe 85-90 for rough-complete
Value: 0 (no numeric reading asked specifically, but could represent slope - but not specified)
Confidence: high, 0.95
Findings: back-graded section causing puddle/standing water
Actions: re-grade the affected section to required slope, retest
Rationale: IPC 704 requires proper slope; back-graded section is non-compliant
References: IPC 704

  ],
  "rationale": "IPC 704 requires horizontal drainage piping to be installed with proper slope toward the point of disposal. A back-graded section that fails a puddle test is non-compliant and defective, requiring correction before the rough-in can be accepted.",
